title: A neural mechanism for terminating decisions

frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decisions accumulate evidence until a threshold triggers commitment

frameworks.name: threshold / bound-crossing mechanism
frameworks.one_line: SC burst implements the threshold sensor applied to LIP accumulation

core_question: Where and how is an evidence-accumulation decision terminated in the primate brain?
key_assumptions: LIP activity represents accumulated evidence on single trials
decisions require a discrete commitment event distinct from accumulation
eye movements report the chosen alternative
manipulations: simultaneous LIP and SC recording during perceptual decisions
key_findings: LIP tracks graded evidence accumulation on each trial
SC produces one burst at decision end, sometimes preceded by smaller bursts
SC inactivation requires a stronger LIP signal to terminate a decision
interpretations: SC bursts act as a threshold sensor reading out LIP accumulation
termination reflects a distinct transformation from deliberation to commitment
